observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Stop granting additional cash advance to concerned officials and employees unless the previous cash advance granted them have been liquidated;

Refrain from granting elective officials cash advances other than their authorized traveling expenses; and

Comply with and strictly observe the laws, rules and regulations regarding cash advance to lessen the accumulation of the same, such that, failure to liquidate within the prescribed period shall cause the suspension of salaries of accountable officers concerned as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-004.

We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of the PPE account of the LGU, specifically, the following:

The Inventory Committee:

Ensure that the physical count is recorded/documented daily in a standard Inventory Count Form (ICF), using the format in Annex A of this Circular, which shall be used as the basis in the preparation of the RPCPPE after the physical count; and

Prepare and update Property records based on the results of the physical inventory and reconcile with accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the LGU's PPEs.

Property and Accounting Unit

The Property and Accounting Units undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the PPELCs maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled. The reconciliation completed within 10 days from rendition of the RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee.

We recommended that management conduct the disposal proceedings necessary for those PPE items that are both fully depreciated and unserviceable, to allow fair presentation of balances in the financial statements.

Also, we recommended that management take heed and observe the updated guidelines on the disposal of government properties set by the newly-issued COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1 to facilitate systematic and timely disposal proceedings which are essential to avoid further deterioration of the properties and consequent depreciation in their value.

We recommended the Management to:

Send a demand letter to the retired Municipal Treasurer requiring her to settle the undeposited balance and report to the Audit Team immediately if deposits have been made by submitting a copy of the deposit slip; and

Ensure that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to reclassify the undeposited balance of the retired Municipal Treasurer from Cash - in Local Treasury account to Other Receivables account to accurately reflect the nature of the transaction in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management ensure the following:

That the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review and validate the unreconciled balance of the CIP account by tracing prior years’ records and transactions, to fully identify and document the composition of the account; and

That the Municipal Engineer submit the Status Report on Government Projects, Programs, and Activities (RGPPAs) to the Municipal Accountant together with the Certificate of Completion, to facilitate the transfer of the total cost of the completed infrastructure project from the CIP account to the appropriate PPE accounts and require the concerned contractors to submit the necessary supporting documents for the processing of their claims on the unbilled portion of the completed projects.

We recommended to Management, through the Municipal Agriculturist, inform and/or report to the GSO and Municipal Accountant all items received from the Department of Agriculture and Phil Rice, as well as the distribution of these items to the intended beneficiaries, to serve as basis of the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the payable accounts, and to furnish the Auditor with a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) reflecting the adjustments. Henceforth, the appropriate accounts should be used in recording transactions in the books, with due consideration of the account descriptions provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts

We recommended that the management ensure that the Municipal Accountant prepares the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the cash advances accounts and to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the adjustments. Henceforth, record and/or classify the cash advances based on the nature of the disbursements with due consideration on the description of the accounts provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management conduct the necessary disposal proceedings for these PPE items that are both fully depreciated and unserviceable, to allow fair presentation of balances in the financial statements and take heed and observe the guidelines on the disposal of government properties as prescribed in Joint Circular No. 2024-1 dated January 30, 2024 to facilitate systematic and timely disposal proceedings which are essential to avoid further deterioration of the properties and consequent depreciation in their value.

We recommended that Management ensure that the:
Municipal Accountant (i) assign a staff who will maintain supplies ledger cards containing the details for each receipt, issuance and remaining balances of all supplies; and
General Services Officer (i) properly maintain and update the stock cards of all inventories;(ii) submit the SSMI regularly to the Accounting Office for proper accounting thereof; and (iii) submit to the office of the Auditor the RPCI as required in Sec. 124 of Chapter 7 of NGAS Volume I.

We recommended that the Management ensure that:
The Municipal Accountant and MEDIPO-Designate investigate and resolve the discrepancies noted, afterwards reconcile and update their respective records to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the balances of Customers’ Deposits Payable account presented in the financial statements; and
The Municipal Accountant makes the necessary adjusting journal entries to transfer the balances of the Customers’ Deposits Payable account from the General Fund to the Trust Fund and to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the adjustments. Henceforth, record and/or classify the deposits received based on the nature or purpose of the funds with due consideration of the provisions of RA No. 7160.

We recommended the following to management:

To direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to set-up a year-end cut-off period of collections in anticipation to series of holidays to be able to deposits it within the year and avoid keeping cash collections for an extended period which makes the fund vulnerable to risk of possible loss though force majeure, misuse or misappropriation;

To follow-up on the undeposited collections of the retired Municipal Treasurer by sending demand letter to the latter and report to COA Audit Team immediately if deposits had been made through the submission of the copy of the deposit slip; and

To adopt necessary control measures in the handling and deposits of cash collections so as not to expose the scarce government monies to the risk of possible loss and misuse.
We recommended that management require the accountable officer to render the report to the COA auditor as prescribed in the above cited provisions.

We recommended that the LCE:

Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to require the Collecting Officer delegated with the task of collecting revenues of the LGU to apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover their cash accountability; and monitor the compliance with the bonding requirement among collectors or AOs.

Strictly avoid designating as Collecting Officer employees with Job Order Status and those who are not bonded to collect; and

Require the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budget for bond premium.

We recommended that management:

require collectors to ensure that collections be remitted or deposited/liquidated intact daily or at the close of each business day and for collectors assigned to the field, where travel time from their places of assignment to the Treasurer’s Office is more than one day, turnover of collections shall be made at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach ₱5,000.00; and

Stop the practice of remitting only after the whole stub of ORs is consumed to avoid the possible misuse of collections or loss through theft or robbery.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant of the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from them as required by Section 20 of the Manual of NGAS, Volume 1;

The Municipal Accountant maintain SLs based on the certified list of taxpayers provided by the Municipal Treasurer to support the reported RPT/SET Receivable control accounts; and

Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant update both their records of any changes in the list of taxpayers to reflect realistic RPT/SET receivables and collections.

We recommended to management:

Direct the Municipal Health Officer to inform and/or report to the GSO and Municipal Accountant all the receipt and distribution of donated vaccines and medicines;

To create an Appraisal Committee to assess the value of these vaccines and medicines still on hand; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the same in the books by debiting Drugs and Medicines for Distribution account and crediting Grants and Donations in Kind account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the previously classified PPE items with carrying amounts from ₱15,000.00 to below ₱50,000.00 as semi-expendable property, subject to the computation of the corresponding impairment. Secondly, tangible items to be acquired in the ensuing years be properly recorded as semi-expendable property.

We also recommended that the GSO use the new forms, registries, and reports prescribed in the Circular, where necessary, to strengthen controls over the semi-expendable items.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive to enjoin the department head’s compliance with the requirements of the circular and direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;

That thereafter, the GSO to prepare and maintain all required reports, records and ledgers as mentioned in COA Circular No. 2015-008; and

Lastly, direct the GSO to reconcile its records with the Accounting Office.

We recommended to management the following:

Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with the responsibilities provided for in paragraph 4.0 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 1 to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes resulting to optimal utilization of the 20% EDF;

MDC with the assistance of the MPDC as head of the secretariat to specifically identify/select, plan and evaluate the PAPs subject for appropriation to ensure utilization of the funds; and

Members of the MDC coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of development programs and projects to ensure that non-implementation of PPAs be avoided as required in Section 109 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Budget Officer properly assess and validate the charging of PPAs to the LDRRMF and ensure that such expenditures are in accordance with the policies and guidelines provided under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 to efficiently utilize the funds and to do away with PAPs that are not related to disaster risk reduction management activities.
We recommended that the GFPS conduct continuous monitoring on the implementation of GAD PAPs identified in the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and all Department Heads to ensure that the list of ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year is prepared with complete details and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year and quarterly period thereafter; and

Inform the Office of the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the infrastructure project's award or before starting the program/activity, that the appropriate project signboards and public notices are already posted.

We recommended that Management:

direct the GFPS to conduct continuous monitoring on the implementation of GAD PAPs identified in the GAD Plan and Budget; and

requires the GAD Focal Point System to resubmit a completely filled-up CY 2023 GAD AR with (a) accompanying brief summary of the reported program or project and (b) copies of report policy issuances, if any.

We recommended to the LCE the following:

Instruct the person responsible to regularly furnish copies of contract of infrastructure projects to enable the local auditor to conduct the necessary legal and auditorial reviews as well as future reference of COA-TAS in the conduct of technical review and inspection pursuant to the guidelines in the Manual on Contracts Review;

Instruct the Bids and Award Committee, BAC-Secretariat and BAC –TWG to ensure the timely preparation and submission of contracts and Purchase Orders and make sure that complete supporting documents were properly complied with every project and make the necessary request for inspection of COA engineer upon completion of projects prior to its final payment; and

Submit to the audit team the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.

We recommended to Management that the Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Point System endorse to the Committee on Family, Women, Children and Elderly of the SB, the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313, which if passed, shall be posted in conspicuous places. Further, include in the ordinance the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions, b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof, and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant for CY 2023 and onwards:

To prepare and submit on time the BRS for a timely verification and audit of the accounts and stop the practice of preparing the latter on batches.

We recommended the following to management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Monthly Trial Balance within 20th day after the end of the month and the Quarterly Financial Statements to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 shall be imposed; and

Submit justification for failure to submit the required the above-mentioned reports.

We recommended the following:

Lastly, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the Infrastructure Assets Accounts, ascertain the estimated useful life used for each component of the asset, determine the proper valuation of the local road assets and compute depreciation to arrive at the correct book value of each component as of December 31, 2022.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant for CY 2022 and onwards:

a) To prepare and submit on time the BRS for a timely verification and audit of the accounts and stop the practice of preparing the latter on batches; and

b) for Landbank accounts, register to weAccess internet banking facility to hasten banking transactions especially in obtaining bank statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the MEDO:

a) 	to facilitate the execution of the lease contracts between the LGU and the stall owners;

b) 	after which, copies of the contracts and all the documents forming part thereof shall be submitted to the Auditor within five working days from the execution, for auditorial review; and

c) 	maintain an individual ledger of the lessee in order to monitor the monthly rental payments of the stall owners.

We recommended that SB members ensure the adoption of appropriate ordinance on every undertaking of the LGU that need such to enhance economic and social development for the protection of peace, safety, health, and good morals of the people. We also recommend that management refrain from carrying out acts without the approval of the SB.

We recommended that:

a) 	the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer implement fully the programs, projects and activities under mitigation fund in accordance with the prescribed guidelines to equip the LGU officials and its constituents the skill and knowledge to prepare and manage disaster;

b) 	the LDRRM Council to monitor the full implementation of the PPAs and properly assess the programs, projects and activities in conformity with the policies and guidelines provided in NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 to avoid disbursements which are not related to disaster risk reduction management activities; and

c) 	the MDC to prioritize those PPAs relative to the utilization of LDRRMF that will equip the MDRRMO with the necessary tools and equipment to make them more effective and efficiently responsive in times of disaster and calamities.
We recommended that management assign a separate Responsibility Center and RC code for the GFPS which shall serve as the RC for GAD-related expenses and GAD-related financial transactions.
We recommended that Management comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all serviceable properties with GSIS.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011, COA Circular No. 2002-003 on spoiled and stale checks, and DBM Circular Letter No. 2008-8 and record adjustments for valid reconciling items. Further, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant:

a)	Send communications to the concerned payees or creditors informing them on the status of the checks to be claimed. If the concerned payees or creditors have claimed the checks, require the latter to return/ present the stale checks and the Municipal Treasurer issue a new check to the claimant; and
 
b)	After exhaustion of the communication phase and there are still unclaimed checks in the possession of the Municipal Treasurer, the Municipal Accountant adjust the accounts thru the following entry:

Cash in Bank		XXX
Accounts Payable/
Other Appropriate Liability Account 	      XXX

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to:

a)	Ensure the completeness of prescribed supporting documents for the granting of cash advances; 

b)	Stop processing cash advances of elected officials other than for official travel; and 


c)	Strictly monitor periodically the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof to avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that non-existing or missing PPE items without available record of accountability be derecognized from the books of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of government agencies to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant reconcile the discrepancies noted and make the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.

We recommended to Local School Board the following:

a.	Submit the above required documents and proof of its submission; and



b. 	Prepare and approve the subsequent SEF Budget in accordance with the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 to attain its goals and objectives and observe transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF.

We recommended to management the following:

a. 	MDC identify and select PPAs which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance to Item 3.0 of the Circular.   

b. 	MPDC as head of the secretariat of the MDC prepare the Individual Project Profile to ensure that the identified PPAs are properly selected, planned and evaluated.

We recommended the following for the optimal utilization of the appropriation for 20% EDF:

a) 	Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with the responsibilities provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.  

b) 	Members of the MDC coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of development programs and projects to ensure non-implementation of PPAs as required in Section 109 of RA No. 7160.

c) 	MDC reprogram all balances pertaining to savings due to 100% completion of PPAs thru identifying new PPAs which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

We recommended that LDRRM Council properly assess the programs, projects and activities in conformity with the policies and guidelines provided in NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 to avoid disbursements which are not related to disaster risk reduction management activities. 

We further recommended that LDRMMO efficiently employ the use of the portable tablets with wifi in relation to its essential function and activities.

13.9	We recommended that the GAD Focal Person monitor the implementation of GAD activities and ensure that GAD fund will be fully utilized to attain its goals and objectives. Moreover, the management through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS):

We recommended that management:
● ceases from granting additional cash advances to officers/employees with unsettled cash advances;
● sends immediate demand letters to employees with outstanding cash advances and require   them to immediately settle the amount granted. If liquidation is still not achieved, consider withholding their salaries or any amount due them until balances have been fully settled; and
● requires the Accountant to monitor outstanding cash advances of every officials & employees and report the status of their liquidations to management.

We recommended that management strictly adheres to COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting cash advances and immediately stop the practice of allowing unbonded officials of the municipality to draw cash advance other than those allowed under the said COA Circular.

We recommended that the LCE follow strictly the guidelines on travel under EO No. 298 and Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents before payment is made. In addition, management is required to refrain from allowing officials to draw another cash advances for travel when previous one was not yet liquidated and in excess of what is legally allowed with a view to preventing excessive and extravagant expenditures in the future.
We further recommended that management stop the practice of allowing partial liquidation of travel cash advance and that refund for excess travel claim must be done first before liquidation is recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that management should be mindful of the guidelines set forth in the circular and submit immediately the documents required in the cited circular for post audit.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer shall be guided by the limitation set forth in JC 2015-01 in the preparation of the budget for confidential expenses to avoid incurrence of excessive confidential expenditures.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer as head of Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant reconcile the discrepancies between the inventory reports and accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant examines the validity of the supporting documents before payment shall be done to avoid possible suspensions and/or disallowance in audit and to strictly comply the computations of benefits as provided in DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, s2012 dated November 29, 2012 as amended by DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, s2016 dated July 15, 2016.

We recommended that management strictly follow the budgetary limitations as provided for under Sec. 325 (a) of R.A. 7160.  Further, institute a strong internal control over the LGU’s transactions to ensure a balanced budget and that fund is allocated to the delivery of basic services and development projects.

The Government Share for HDMF contributions of LGU employees shall be restored back to ₱100.00 per month. However, the employees may continue to contribute more than ₱100.00 provided that their Net Take Home Pay shall not be less than ₱4,000.00.

We recommended that management strictly implements the provisions of DSWD Department Order No. 22, as amended by Administrative Order No. 5, Series of 2008, and Section 4(2 & 6) of P. D. 1445. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from post-audit of transactions.

We recommended that an Internal Audit Service unit be established in the Municipality of Tigbao, following the requirement under Budget Circular No. 2004-4. It should be under the direct administrative supervision and control of the head of the agency and should be organized as an independent staff unit to perform staff functions and to institute and conduct a program of internal audit for the Municipality of Tigbao.
Make representation to the Sangguniang Bayan, for the due establishment/creation of the Internal Audit Service unit, wherein the proposal should include the organizational structure, staffing, functional charts (departmental and individual), operating budget and funding source. In organizing the unit, reference should be made to DBM budget Circular No. 2004-4 (Guidelines on the Organization and Staffing of Internal Auditing Units), the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume III (Government Auditing Standards and Procedures and Internal Control System) and the National Guidelines on Internal Control Systems, which was made effective under DBM Circular Letter no. 2008-8.

We recommended that the LCE, the Local Development Council, and the Local Finance Committee be guided by the limitations set forth in the JMC No. 2011-01 in the preparation of the budget for 20% EDF and shall refrain from allocating funds for Public Order and Safety Programs under the 20% EDF.

We recommended that the management refrain from allocating funds for confidential expenses under the LDRRM funds. The fund has its specific purposes enumerated under Par. 3.0 of COA Circular 2012-002 which shall be used to support disaster risk management activities/ programs.

We recommended that the LSB refrains from appropriating for non-priority expenditures in the SEF budget, especially for those that are part of the regular activities of schools, and/or the DepEd. Instead, it will allocate the Fund for the construction and repair of school buildings; facilities and equipment; educational research; and acquisition of books and periodicals. Furthermore, the LSB shall evaluate the programs/projects/activities it ought to include in the SEF budget while taking into consideration the objectives set forth in RA 7160 and RA 5447.

We recommended that the LGU employees involved in the approval and payment of the transactions should examine the completeness of the supporting documents to ensure their validity, propriety and legality.

We recommended that the LCE directs the BAC to thoroughly review the procurement process and adopt the correct procedures under negotiated procurement as an alternative method of procurement due to two failed biddings.

We recommended that the BAC follow the guidelines as provided in Annex H of RIRR of RA 9184 relative to Small Value Procurement to avail the most advantageous price in favor of the Municipal government.
We recommended that the management strengthen the review process and accounting controls over transactions involving payment of meals and snacks during meetings and other official activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant sees to it that all requests for the procurement of medicines should be supported with a certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF, Volume I.  Moreover, LCE and all officers concerned to strictly adhere to the RIRR of RA 9184 in their procurement process. Also, future transactions relating documentary requirements enumerated in the COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 be completely attached to the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the procurement section to prioritize acquisition of regular or ordinary office supplies through PS-DBM before resorting to public bidding.
In case of non-availability of stocks, attach to the disbursement vouchers the Agency Procurement Request duly acted by the PS-DBM and the Price Quotation and Stocks Availability Certification on the items which are available on stocks. In case of non-availability of stocks, check for the notation on the agency procurement request the information either out of stock or that the items are non-common office supplies.
We recommended that the LGU appropriates the correct amount for the strengthening and implementation of LCPC as required under Section 15 of RA 9344.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person, along with responsible officials, strictly comply with the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 thru conducting of gender analysis and assessment so that they can formulate policies and plans which can address gender issues in their LGU and mainstream the programs, projects and activities that could solve the issues identified to attain gender equality.

We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board should fully comply with RA 9003 and implement projects to be undertaken under the Municipal revised SMWP.

We recommended that management instructs the Accounting Department to follow strictly the provision on the monthly Take Home Pay of all the regular municipal employees to ₱3,000.00 and to adopt control measures in the issuance of Certification for loan eligibility.

We recommended that management develop and pass a GAD Code based on the gender issues and concerns in its locality and on the women’s empowerment and gender equality

agenda of the government.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) as the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) should reorganize the BAC composition to conform with the above-cited provisions of law.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adopts internal control measures to preclude the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures in the form of surcharges on electric consumption.

We recommended that management shall cease from allowing transactions to be processed and paid without the approval of proper officials who shall be responsible for its compliance with laws, rules and regulations. In order not to hamper the operations of the agency, management may designate an alternate signatory who shall sign in behalf of an official in case of his absence.
